response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang backgrounds, but the role is an embedded networking security position requiring native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and Linux kernel source-modification experience — none of which are evidenced. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and there is no demonstrated experience with L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded firmware for network devices. The language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and low-latency systems work in DLang
Linux proficiency and some C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
No embedded networking or security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips, Linux kernel modification)
C programming experience is dated and brief; career trajectory is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: General engineering seniority and Linux background
